title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: The game has a hard time launching. If you delete the logs ( ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/342200/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Saved Games/MechWarrior Online/  -  wipe the whole folder ) you can get to the login page. I've logging in works and start to load the mechlab, but it crashes frequently. So you have to delete logs and try again. After about 20 tries or so, I was able to get into a game. From there the game runs perfectly! It's damn near native! As others have mentioned in the MWO forums ( https://mwomercs.com/forums/topic/268847-running-the-game-on-ubuntu-steam-play/ ), user settings won't save and there are some other minor issues. If you try with enough, you can get a game or two in.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: The game itself runs fine once you pass a number of possible CTDs related to an outdated version of the boost library used, details on these crashes are reported here: https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=46022. A possible fix, though somewhat hack-y, can be found here: https://mwomercs.com/forums/topic/268847-running-the-game-on-ubuntu-steam-play/page__view__findpost__p__6195387. It should be noted that this only uses steam play to install the game, actually running it requires compiling wine from source after editing a file, then using this version on wine on the prefix prepared by steam play.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Downloaded the game through Proton and allowed it to create a prefix, but had to compile a custom wine-staging-nine with a patch to prevent crashing due to an outdated Boost library. Submitted detailed instructions on winehq.org, which is awaiting review.
